title: Return meaningful exit codes
description: Exit 0 only on success and use distinct non-zero codes for distinct failure classes so scripts and CI can branch on them.
body: Use when a CLI can succeed or fail. Do not return 0 after an error, and do not print 'error' to the screen while exiting 0. Actions: exit 0 on success, reserve non-zero codes for failure classes (e.g. 1 general error, 2 usage error), document them, and ensure every error path sets the code before exit. Validation signals include `tool; echo $?` showing 0 on success and the documented non-zero on each failure, and CI correctly detecting failures. Risk boundary: an exit 0 on failure makes a broken step look green in CI and silently passes bad data downstream — a wrong exit code is a real defect.
